headline: AMD, Cerebras Strike AI Chip Deals
summary: Advanced Micro Devices and Cerebras Systems on Wednesday separately announced partnerships related to their AI chips. AMD stock and Cerebras stock rose on the news as semiconductor stocks overall advanced.

headline: PANW to Buy Embrace: Will it Strengthen the Observability Platform?
summary: Palo Alto Networks is expanding its Observability platform with Embrace and Synthetics to strengthen digital experience monitoring and AI-driven operations.

headline: CrowdStrike Just Gave Cerebras a Big Vote of Confidence
summary: CrowdStrike will use Cerebras’ artificial-intelligence chips to help power its Falcon AI Detection and Response, or AIDR, platform.

headline: Jim Cramer Calls AI Cyberattacks a “Watershed Moment” for CrowdStrike
summary: Jim Cramer says machine-on-machine attacks have crossed a threshold that one cybersecurity company saw coming long before Wall Street did, and the financial results suggest the market is finally catching up.

raw.headline: Jim Cramer Calls AI Cyberattacks a “Watershed Moment” for CrowdStrike

headline: OpenAI hack: For 'every dollar' spent on AI, another should be spent on cybersecurity
summary: Simplify Asset Management managing director and head of multi-asset solutions, Paisley Nardini, and Goldman Sachs Asset Management chief investment strategist, Tim Urbanowicz, discuss the importance of cybersecurity in the AI age, especially in light of OpenAI's (OPAI.PVT) recent hack.

headline: Is CrowdStrike Holdings (CRWD) Cheap On Its Schwarz Digits Partnership And Pullback?
summary: CrowdStrike Holdings (CRWD) has drawn fresh attention after expanding its partnership with Schwarz Digits to bring the Falcon cybersecurity platform to European enterprises on STACKIT, a sovereign cloud operated within the European Union. See our latest analysis for CrowdStrike Holdings. The latest Schwarz Digits agreement and recent product developments come after a sharp pullback, with CrowdStrike Holdings’ share price down 9.3% over the past week but still posting a 68.6% year to date...
